zh.Elmington, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾艾爾明頓
zh.lifeSummary: Mary Alice Eddy於1443年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾艾爾明頓。她於1463年與Thomas Lovingott II在英國奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1500年在英國奧可福爾享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Eddy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cornish: from the personal name Edy (pronounced ‘eedy’), a variant of Udy , from the Middle English personal name Ude, Udy, Latinized as Udo and Odo. It may represent Old French Eude (ancient Germanic Eudo, of uncertain etymology), whose usual Latin form is Eudo. This agrees with later evidence that the original pronunciation of the initial vowel of Udy was /y:/ (as in French tu), though in the 16th century it was sometimes unrounded to /i:/, spelled -e(e)-. It was later altered to Eddy.
English: perhaps from a shortened form of the Middle English personal name Edwy (Old English Ēadwīg, from ēad ‘prosperity, fortune’ + wīg ‘war’), which has not survived in that form as a surname.

variants.name: Udy
meanings: Cornish and English (Cornwall): of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal name Ude, Udy (Cornish Edy), Latinized as Udo and Odo. It may represent Old French Eude (ancient Germanic Eudo, of uncertain etymology), whose usual Latin form is Eudo. This agrees with later evidence that the original pronunciation of the initial vowel of Udy was /y:/ (as in French tu). Compare Eddy .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Alice Eddy was born in 1443, in Elmington, Northamptonshire, England. She married Thomas Lovingott II in 1463, in Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1500, in Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 57.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Alice Eddyは1443年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアエルミントンで生まれた。1463年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでThomas Lovingott IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1500年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで57歳で死去した。

ko.loveday, elmington, oxford: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 더비 스퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Alice Eddy(은)는 1443년 영국 노르섬프토실르 엘밍턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1463년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 Thomas Lovingott II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1500년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mary Alice Eddy родилась в 1443 г. в местности элмингтон, нортамптоншир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Lovingott II в 1463 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1500 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.
